content: Generalized Anxiety Disorder (GAD) is characterized by excessive, uncontrollable worry about multiple events or activities occurring more days than not for at least 6 months. The anxiety and worry are associated with ≥3 physical/cognitive symptoms: restlessness, fatigue, difficulty concentrating, irritability, muscle tension, or sleep disturbance. Symptoms cause clinically significant distress/impairment and are not attributable to substances, medical conditions, or other mental disorders. GAD has a lifetime prevalence of ~5-6%, with female predominance (2:1). Pathophysiology involves amygdala hyperactivity, prefrontal cortex dysregulation, GABAergic dysfunction, and genetic factors (heritability ~30%).
